cloudflare-worker-deploy.description: Deploy a Cloudflare Worker via wrangler with env-aware secrets. Covers wrangler.jsonc selection, secret bindings, route verification with wrangler tail, and OpenNext deploys.

git-workflow.description: Commit, branch, and PR conventions: small commits with conventional messages, branches named with a type/scope prefix, PRs that fit on one screen and pass CI before requesting review.

vitest-runner.description: Run vitest with the project's own config. Prefer vitest run over watch mode during agent sessions, use --reporter=basic for compact output, and read first-failure-first when triaging.

mcp-server-debug.description: Diagnose an MCP server that is failing to register tools or returning errors. Walks through stdio handshake, env-var injection, tool-schema introspection, and host-side debugging.

next-15-app-router.description: Patterns for Next.js 15 App Router projects — server vs. client components, route handlers, async params (Next 15 change), revalidation, and metadata.

frontend-design.description: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces that avoid generic AI aesthetics. Covers typography, color, motion, spatial composition, and visual depth. Use when building components, pages, dashboards, or styling any web UI.

code-review-and-quality.description: Multi-axis code review playbook: correctness, readability, architecture, security, performance. Use before merging any change, including code written by yourself or another agent.

debugging-and-error-recovery.description: Systematic root-cause debugging. Use when tests fail, builds break, or behavior doesn't match expectations. Replaces guess-and-check with reproduce → narrow → fix → verify.

code-simplification.description: Simplify code for clarity without changing behavior. Use when refactoring code that has accumulated unnecessary complexity, dead branches, premature abstractions, or compatibility shims.

test-driven-development.description: Drive development with tests: write the failing test before the code, reproduce a bug with a test before fixing it. Use when implementing any logic, fixing any bug, or changing any behavior.
